product_name: SJ - Figure Skating Gym Drop-In (30 Minutes)
description:
Skaters and Coaches
 Figure Skating Gym is for teaching and training. Anyone participating in gym activities must adhere to all rules and etiquette. The director of skating reserves the right to monitor all gym sessions and make appropriate changes.
 Participants must register online and check-in at the front desk before entering the gym.
 Anyone who has not paid will not be allowed access to the gym.
 Gym use is only permitted with a coach present.
 Do Not Enter before session time.
 Gym equipment is for training only all equipment must be used for intended use.
 Only one participant allowed on a piece of equipment at one time.
 Abusive or obscene language/music will not be tolerated.
 All skaters and coaches must pick up tissues, garbage, water bottles etc. after their session.
 All session times are subject to change. Reasonable notice of changes will be posted whenever possible.
 No food or drink is allowed except for water or sport drinks kept in a closed container.
 Refrain from comments or actions that are disruptive, offensive, abusive, or racist. Behavior that constitutes harassment or abuse we are not tolerated by Sharks Ice.
 All participants should have equal opportunity to equipment. Be courteous to one another and allow everyone a turn on equipment.
facility: Sharks Ice at San Jose
address: 1500 South Tenth Street, San Jose, CA 95112

 Participants must register online and check-in at the front desk before entering the gym.
 Anyone who has not paid will not be allowed access to the gym.
 Gym use is only permitted with a coach present.
 Do Not Enter before session time.
 Gym equipment is for training only all equipment must be used for intended use.
 Only one participant allowed on a piece of equipment at one time.
 Abusive or obscene language/music will not be tolerated.
 All skaters and coaches must pick up tissues, garbage, water bottles etc. after their session.
 All session times are subject to change. Reasonable notice of changes will be posted whenever possible.
 No food or drink is allowed except for water or sport drinks kept in a closed container.
 Refrain from comments or actions that are disruptive, offensive, abusive, or racist. Behavior that constitutes harassment or abuse we are not tolerated by Sharks Ice.
 All participants should have equal opportunity to equipment. Be courteous to one another and allow everyone a turn on equipment.

description:
This class will focus on developing the skating skills of the LTS Intermediate and Advanced Adult student who seeks more expertise and strength in edge quality and turns. The curriculum will include stroking, crossovers, chasses, progressives, swing rolls and change edge as well as C-turns (Mohawks) and three turns. It will better position the student to further their exploration into advanced skating disciplines includingFigures, LTS Advanced, Dance and Synchro.
Successful completion of this series will meet the prerequisite for Ice Dance One.
Prerequisite: Minimum One series of Intermediate Classes or equivalent

description: Intense Dynamic Hockey Training (IDHT) AA or interested AA players IDHT will encompass intense dynamic training sessions in a highly competitive environment focused on the development and enjoyment of high-level players. Players will be invited to participate in IDHT via selection by the coaching staff through application process. Players interested in participating in 14U, or 16U/18U/Junior and Prep Intense Dynamic Hockey Training (IDHT) sessions must complete the IDHT Player Profile  Interest Form. Profiles will be reviewed by coaches and players will be invited to attend after reviewed. IDHT Birth Years 2026: 14U: 2013  2012 16U: 2011  2010 18U/ JR/ Prep: 2009 +

description:
Ice Dance 1
(Formerly Introduction to Ice Dance)
30 minute class followed by 90 minute Ice Dance session
Open to children, youth and adults

Requirements:
Advanced level in the Learn To Skate program; Stroking, forward and
backward cross overs, all edges, outside three turns and inside C-turns (Mohawks)

Instructors will introduce Ice Dance elements: swing rolls, progressives and chasses. Teach how to read dance diagrams for steps, patterns and timing. Teach the preliminary dances, introduce ice dance timing and expression (ex: tango vs waltz). Teach how to safely participate in Ice Dance practice and training sessions
facility: Oakland Ice Center Operated by Sharks Ice

description:
Ice Dance 2:
30 minute class followed by 90 minute Ice Dance session
Open to children, youth and adults

Prerequisite is Ice Dance 1 or USFS tests at the preliminary dance level

Instructors will continue per Ice Dance 1, refining and strengthening all elements teach the pre-bronze dances and per instructors discretion, the bronze
dances teach safety and etiquette for Ice Dance Social Sessions.

product_name: SJ - Figure Skating Gym Drop-In (30 Minutes)
description:
Skaters and Coaches
 Figure Skating Gym is for teaching and training. Anyone participating in gym activities must adhere to all rules and etiquette. The director of skating reserves the right to monitor all gym sessions and make appropriate changes.
 Participants must register online and check-in at the front desk before entering the gym.
 Anyone who has not paid will not be allowed access to the gym.
 Gym use is only permitted with a coach present.
 Do Not Enter before session time.
 Gym equipment is for training only all equipment must be used for intended use.
 Only one participant allowed on a piece of equipment at one time.
 Abusive or obscene language/music will not be tolerated.
 All skaters and coaches must pick up tissues, garbage, water bottles etc. after their session.
 All session times are subject to change. Reasonable notice of changes will be posted whenever possible.
 No food or drink is allowed except for water or sport drinks kept in a closed container.
 Refrain from comments or actions that are disruptive, offensive, abusive, or racist. Behavior that constitutes harassment or abuse we are not tolerated by Sharks Ice.
 All participants should have equal opportunity to equipment. Be courteous to one another and allow everyone a turn on equipment.
facility: Sharks Ice at San Jose

 Anyone who has not paid will not be allowed access to the gym.
 Gym use is only permitted with a coach present.
 Do Not Enter before session time.
 Gym equipment is for training only all equipment must be used for intended use.
 Only one participant allowed on a piece of equipment at one time.
 Abusive or obscene language/music will not be tolerated.
 All skaters and coaches must pick up tissues, garbage, water bottles etc. after their session.
 All session times are subject to change. Reasonable notice of changes will be posted whenever possible.
 No food or drink is allowed except for water or sport drinks kept in a closed container.
 Refrain from comments or actions that are disruptive, offensive, abusive, or racist. Behavior that constitutes harassment or abuse we are not tolerated by Sharks Ice.
 All participants should have equal opportunity to equipment. Be courteous to one another and allow everyone a turn on equipment.

Introducing show/performance and synchronized skating for our youth learn to skate participants. Synchronized skating is a fun way for all skaters at any level to improve edge quality, power, speed, and flexibility. Prerequisite: forward snowplow stop.Performances: TBD

description:
Skaters and Coaches
 Figure Skating Gym is for teaching and training. Anyone participating in gym activities must adhere to all rules and etiquette. The director of skating reserves the right to monitor all gym sessions and make appropriate changes.
 Participants must register online and check-in at the front desk before entering the gym.

product_name: SJ - Figure Skating Gym Drop-In (30 Minutes)
description:
Skaters and Coaches
 Figure Skating Gym is for teaching and training. Anyone participating in gym activities must adhere to all rules and etiquette. The director of skating reserves the right to monitor all gym sessions and make appropriate changes.
 Participants must register online and check-in at the front desk before entering the gym.
 Anyone who has not paid will not be allowed access to the gym.
 Gym use is only permitted with a coach present.
 Do Not Enter before session time.
 Gym equipment is for training only all equipment must be used for intended use.
 Only one participant allowed on a piece of equipment at one time.
 Abusive or obscene language/music will not be tolerated.
 All skaters and coaches must pick up tissues, garbage, water bottles etc. after their session.
 All session times are subject to change. Reasonable notice of changes will be posted whenever possible.
 No food or drink is allowed except for water or sport drinks kept in a closed container.
 Refrain from comments or actions that are disruptive, offensive, abusive, or racist. Behavior that constitutes harassment or abuse we are not tolerated by Sharks Ice.
 All participants should have equal opportunity to equipment. Be courteous to one another and allow everyone a turn on equipment.

address: 1500 South Tenth Street, San Jose, CA 95112

product_name: SJ - Figure Skating Gym Drop-In (1 Hour)
description:
Skaters and Coaches
 Figure Skating Gym is for teaching and training. Anyone participating in gym activities must adhere to all rules and etiquette. The director of skating reserves the right to monitor all gym sessions and make appropriate changes.
 Participants must register online and check-in at the front desk before entering the gym.
 Anyone who has not paid will not be allowed access to the gym.
 Gym use is only permitted with a coach present.
 Do Not Enter before session time.
 Gym equipment is for training only all equipment must be used for intended use.
 Only one participant allowed on a piece of equipment at one time.
 Abusive or obscene language/music will not be tolerated.
 All skaters and coaches must pick up tissues, garbage, water bottles etc. after their session.
 All session times are subject to change. Reasonable notice of changes will be posted whenever possible.
 No food or drink is allowed except for water or sport drinks kept in a closed container.
 Refrain from comments or actions that are disruptive, offensive, abusive, or racist. Behavior that constitutes harassment or abuse we are not tolerated by Sharks Ice.
 All participants should have equal opportunity to equipment. Be courteous to one another and allow everyone a turn on equipment.
facility: Sharks Ice at San Jose
address: 1500 South Tenth Street, San Jose, CA 95112

facility: Oakland Ice Center Operated by Sharks Ice

description: This Stick  Puck hour is designed for players to just skate and work on any aspect of hockey he or she desires. And most importantly to have fun! Each player is required to sign in before each session at front desk to recieve wristband/sticker. Full USA Hockey Certified equipment required.Locker Rooms are Coed, please plan accordingly. Scrimmages or small area games are not allowed! Advanced registration is requiredLimit 40 skaters and 4 goalies. All Gretzky Hours after 9pm will only be available for online registration. No in person registration.No Refunds.
facility: Sharks Ice at San Jose
address: 1500 South Tenth Street, San Jose, CA 95112
